signals.reasoning: BTC is the cleanest actual long on the board with negative funding, a trade-grade mechanical score, and price/OI still reading trend-supported. With the board clear again, it is back above the bar for a fresh long.
ETH still has supportive negative funding, but the move continues to look more like short-covering than a clean new trend leg and crowding remains elevated. Not as clean as BTC for a fresh buy.
SOL has softened into a more neutral short-covering profile and no longer has enough edge to justify a new long. Watch-only.
HEMI is still too crowded and too strong in the wrong direction to short cleanly, with very positive funding but trend-supported price action. Too awkward either way.

TST keeps one of the clearer bearish reads on the board with a strongly negative mechanical score and a long-liquidation-flush pattern. It is still a smaller alt, so confidence stays moderate, but it is a valid short lean.

BIO still has one of the strongest raw squeeze profiles with deeply negative funding and a trade-grade mechanical score. It remains thinner and already somewhat extended, so it stays below BTC in confidence, but it is still strong enough to upgrade.
VVV has a mildly negative read, but the tape still looks too noisy to trust as a clean short after weakness. Not strong enough to act on.
MON is mildly constructive but not differentiated enough to justify a fresh signal. Watch-only.
FARTCOIN still looks more like recovery participation than a clean new edge. It does not outrank the stronger setups on the board.
GRIFFAIN remains one of the clearest bearish leans with a strongly negative mechanical score and actual relative weakness. It is still thin, but it is one of the better short candidates available.
MERL still has constructive recovery structure, but positive funding and elevated cascade risk keep it below the bar for a clean fresh long. Watch-only.
